fault_hypothesis: Lines 80-81 incorrectly pop the last element when both streams end with the same version, which violates the documented behavior that both copies must be kept. This post-processing block should not exist.

constraints.intent: The pop() call on line 81 should never execute because it incorrectly removes a valid duplicate entry from the merged result.
After the conditional pop, the merged list length must still equal the sum of both input lengths.
After initialization merged__1 is empty; this baseline check ensures the variable is correctly initialized before the loop.
The returned merged list must contain all elements from both input streams without any removals.
